question_html:
Make meaningful sentences with the following words:

Ubiquitous, Scrutinize, Mitigate, Benevolent, Incontrovertible
solution_html:
Ubiquitous – Smartphones have become ubiquitous, found in the hands of people worldwide.

Scrutinize – The auditor will scrutinize the company’s financial records for any irregularities.

Mitigate – Wearing masks helps mitigate the spread of infectious diseases.

Benevolent – The benevolent donor contributed generously to the charity fund.

Incontrovertible – The DNA evidence provided incontrovertible Proof of his innocence.

Marks Distribution:

For making meaningful sentences with the given words, (0.5×5) =2.5 marks.

question_html:
Read the following extract and answer questions that follow:

I wandered lonely as a cloud

That floats on high o’er vales and hills,

When all at once I saw a crowd,

A host, of golden daffodils;

Beside the lake, beneath the trees,

Fluttering and dancing in the breeze.

(a) Identify and explain any one literary device used in the extract.

(b) How does the imagery of the “host of golden daffodils” contribute to the poem’s emotional tone?
solution_html:
(a) Wordsworth uses personification by describing the daffodils as “fluttering and dancing.” This human-like quality creates a vivid, joyful image that mirrors the poet’s lifted spirits.

(b) The image of countless bright, dancing daffodils evokes a feeling of beauty and elation. It transforms the poet’s loneliness into joy, reflecting the restorative power of nature.

Marks Distribution:

For writing, Wordsworth uses personification by describing the daffodils as “fluttering and dancing.” This human-like quality creates a vivid, joyful image that mirrors the poet’s lifted spirits; 1.5 marks.

For writing, the image of countless bright, dancing daffodils evokes a feeling of beauty and elation. It transforms the poet’s loneliness into joy, reflecting the restorative power of nature; 01 mark.

[N.B: 0.5 mark will be deducted for two grammar or spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more grammar or spelling mistakes.]

question_html: Write a paragraph in approximately 100 words analyzing whether technology in education helps reduce inequality or unintentionally widens the digital divide. Present your ideas with a clear argument and supporting evidence.
solution_html:
Digital Learning and the Inequality Dilemma

Technology promised to democratize education, but its results remain mixed. On one hand, digital tools offer access to vast resources, interactive learning, and global classrooms, benefiting many students regardless of location. However, for millions without stable internet or devices, the digital revolution has deepened inequality. In rural or underprivileged communities, technology often highlights the gap rather than bridging it. During the pandemic, some students thrived in virtual classrooms, while others were left behind entirely. The effectiveness of educational technology depends on equitable access, teacher training, and infrastructure development. When used thoughtfully and inclusively, technology can empower learners and enhance education. However, if gaps in access persist, it risks reinforcing existing social and educational divides. Therefore, policymakers must ensure that digital learning lifts all students and does not benefit only the privileged.

Marks Distribution:

For writing the Title “Digital Learning and the Inequality Dilemma”; 0.5 mark.

For writing; Introductory sentence; 01 mark.

For writing; On one hand, digital tools offer access to vast resources, interactive learning, and global classrooms, benefiting many students regardless of location. However, for millions without stable internet or devices, the digital revolution has deepened inequality; 01 mark.

For writing; In rural or underprivileged communities, technology often highlights the gap rather than bridging it. During the pandemic, some students thrived in virtual classrooms, while others were left behind entirely; 01 mark.

For writing; When used thoughtfully and inclusively, technology can empower learners and enhance education; 01 mark.

For writing conclusion; 0.5 mark.

[Instructions:

* Students must write it within one single para. If it becomes more than one para, 50% marks have to be deducted from the obtained marks.

* 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two grammar or spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more grammar or spelling mistakes.]

[N.B. The student will get the allotted marks if he/she writes a proper alternative title.]
